snippets.license: Database for Modern Standard Arabic E-mail this message to a friend Arabic WordNet 4.0 is now available - a large-scale lexical database for Modern Standard Arabic released under CC BY 4.0. snippets.methodology: ) synsets = wn.synsets(lang='arb') METHODOLOGY =========== Arabic WordNet 4.0 was created using the expand approach, machine translating Open English WordNet into Arabic using Gemini 3 Pro Preview. candidates.name: Arabic Ontology
candidates.primary_signal: SinaLab resources listing marks Arabic Ontology as CC-BY-4.0.
candidates.why: Potentially useful for concept/topic coverage, but needs a source card and artifact license capture first.

candidates.name: SinaLab Arabic Synonyms Dataset
candidates.primary_signal: SinaLab resources listing marks Synonyms dataset as CC-BY-4.0.
candidates.why: Good candidate for semantic alternatives/eval; still not dialogue corpus and not tokenizer vocab.

candidates.name: SALMA Arabic Sense Annotated Corpus
candidates.why: Useful for WSD/eval, but it is text/corpus-like and must not enter dialogue training here.

candidates.name: Arabic WordNet 4.0
candidates.primary_signal: LINGUIST announcement says CC-BY-4.0, but methodology says Gemini 3 Pro Preview.
candidates.why: Large and permissively licensed, but generated through external AI, so it conflicts with SF-native data purity.

candidates.name: Open Multilingual Wordnet Arabic WordNet v2
candidates.primary_signal: OMW lists Arabic WordNet v2 as CC BY SA 3.0.
candidates.why: Redistributable, but share-alike is not clean enough for unrestricted SF lexicon import.

candidates.name: Qabas Lexicon
candidates.why: No-derivatives and conflicting metadata block raw entries and derived candidate terms.

decision.next_phase: Phase 27.114 — Arabic Ontology/Synonyms Source Cards and License Matrix, no training
